---
title: "00 · Introduction"
subtitle: "Tier 5 — Undergraduate Core · Years 1–2 · Linear algebra, multivariable calculus, probability, discrete maths, number theory, and the first taste of rigorous analysis"
---

## What this tier is for

Tier 4 made you fluent in calculus and vectors. Tier 5 takes you to
the **undergraduate core** of applied and pure mathematics — the
toolkit every researcher and serious practitioner shares.

This is the tier where mathematics begins to pay off in undisguised
form: linear algebra runs every neural network and 3D engine; partial
derivatives are the mechanics of optimisation; the central limit
theorem governs every statistical claim; sets and logic provide the
language of proof; modular arithmetic powers RSA; and real analysis
gives you the rigorous foundation calculus rests on.

By the end of Tier 5 you can:

- Manipulate **matrices** and solve linear systems by **Gaussian
  elimination**.
- Reason about **vector spaces**, basis, and **eigenvalues**.
- Take **partial derivatives**, compute **gradients** and **multiple
  integrals**.
- Use **vector fields**, **divergence**, and **curl**.
- Work with **continuous probability distributions**, **variance**,
  and the **Central Limit Theorem**.
- Conduct basic **statistical inference** (confidence intervals,
  hypothesis tests).
- Write rigorous **proofs** using sets, logic, induction, and
  contradiction.
- Reason about **graphs** algorithmically.
- Implement and break **RSA** using modular arithmetic.
- Define and use **convergence** of sequences and series rigorously.
- Read the **$\epsilon$-$\delta$** style of real analysis.

## The lessons

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[01 · Matrices] --> B[02 · Linear Systems]
    A --> C[03 · Vector Spaces]
    B --> D[04 · Eigenvalues]
    C --> D
    E[05 · Partial Derivatives] --> F[06 · Multiple Integrals]
    E --> G[07 · Vector Fields]
    H[08 · Continuous Distributions] --> I[09 · Variance & CLT]
    I --> J[10 · Statistical Inference]
    K[11 · Sets, Logic, Proof] --> L[12 · Graphs]
    M[13 · Modular Arithmetic & RSA]
    N[14 · Sequences & Convergence] --> O[15 · Real Analysis]
```

## Lesson list

1. [**01 · Matrices and Matrix Operations**](01-matrices/index.qmd)
2. [**02 · Linear Systems and Gaussian Elimination**](02-linear-systems-gaussian/index.qmd)
3. [**03 · Vector Spaces, Linear Independence, Basis**](03-vector-spaces/index.qmd)
4. [**04 · Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors**](04-eigenvalues/index.qmd)
5. [**05 · Partial Derivatives and the Gradient**](05-partial-derivatives/index.qmd)
6. [**06 · Multiple Integrals**](06-multiple-integrals/index.qmd)
7. [**07 · Vector Fields, Divergence, Curl**](07-vector-fields/index.qmd)
8. [**08 · Continuous Distributions and the Normal**](08-continuous-distributions/index.qmd)
9. [**09 · Variance, Standard Deviation, and the CLT**](09-variance-clt/index.qmd)
10. [**10 · Statistical Inference**](10-statistical-inference/index.qmd)
11. [**11 · Sets, Logic, and Proof Techniques**](11-sets-logic-proofs/index.qmd)
12. [**12 · Graphs and Their Algorithms**](12-graphs-algorithms/index.qmd)
13. [**13 · Modular Arithmetic and the RSA Algorithm**](13-modular-arithmetic-rsa/index.qmd)
14. [**14 · Sequences, Series, and Convergence**](14-sequences-series-convergence/index.qmd)
15. [**15 · Continuity and Differentiability — Rigorous Foundation**](15-real-analysis-foundation/index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note}
This page reflects the snapshot of `_curriculum.yml` at the time of build.
Lesson statuses there are the source of truth.
:::
